refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple obligations or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency
Use a dedicated communication channel with latency monitoring and adaptive control
Incorporate predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency

potential_sources: Tightly coupled communication and control logic could increase latency
Shared resources between teleoperation and other system functions may cause delays
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a modular design with prioritized data handling supports independence between communication and control functions, reducing coupling and enabling latency optimization.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive control algorithms may be needed if latency approaches upper limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency compensation mechanisms in the DP to enhance robustness against variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control system including prioritized data handling to minimize latency

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for vehicle state and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions from operator to teleoperation system

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for remote control and data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion and low cohesion due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Vehicle module mainly handles physical vehicle functions; teleoperation control logic would diffuse heavily outside this boundary.
Highly cohesive containment of teleoperation control system and prioritized data handling; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; teleoperation control logic would be poorly contained here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary that can contain the DP of designing a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, minimizing diffusion and coupling outside this module.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time telemetry and control feedback.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect latency and system responsiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular teleoperation control system with prioritized data handling to minimize latency, directly targeting the requirement for end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation and meeting the acceptance criteria of p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time telemetry and control feedback.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect latency and system responsiveness.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP within this IM boundary. This supports effective architectural packaging and ownership, minimizing diffusion risks compared to broader or unrelated modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions and monitoring operational policies related to worksite safety plan.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation module is clear and specific, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) supporting the functional requirement and enabling traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity with the Vehicle module to reduce potential latency impacts and coupling.
Ensure robust handling and fallback strategies for Network Link interface to maintain latency requirements under variable network conditions.
Consider documenting detailed interface contracts and performance requirements between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to mitigate diffusion risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The Teleoperation module shows strong cohesion and minimal diffusion, and verification feasibility is confirmed. Although some diffusion risks and interface complexities are noted, these are manageable through monitoring and documentation rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and traceability at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: To address potential interface complexity and diffusion risks, it is recommended to document detailed interface contracts and performance requirements between the Teleoperation and Vehicle modules, and to ensure robust handling of the Network Link interface.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently meets the FR with acceptable latency under defined network conditions.
The Teleoperation module's high containment and low diffusion scores indicate effective architectural packaging.
Verification methods for latency and operational policies are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Interface complexity with Vehicle module may impact latency and coupling.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability could affect system responsiveness.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational conditions to confirm p95 ≤ 150 ms.
Documentation of interface contracts and performance requirements between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Monitoring reports on Network Link interface performance and fallback strategy effectiveness.
